commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b3fc0c2710c42d19e58fc532acd6b89c8a693f0c)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Telegram active-run ingress lane for direct and isolated polling text follow-ups, documents it, adds tests, and adds a runtime fallback for legacy bare root-key file SecretRefs.

PR surface: Source +159, Tests +176, Docs 0. Total +335 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows callback_query.message participates in active-run text candidate selection, and the runner contract makes distinct lane keys concurrent. I did not run local tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is needed because one blocker is a product/compatibility decision about whether runtime should accept legacy file SecretRef ids or require doctor migration.

target.title: [codex] Fix Telegram active-run ingress and legacy file SecretRefs
target.body:
## Summary
- route plain-text Telegram follow-ups in an active reply fence through a narrow ingress lane so they can reach queue/steer handling without waiting behind the running handler
- share that active-run lane selection between the direct grammY runner and isolated-spool polling drain path
- keep commands, media, and media-group Telegram updates on the normal serialized lane for compatibility
- keep Telegram sequential-key typing structural around the fields it actually reads, so real bot contexts and isolated-spool/test contexts stay type-compatible
- resolve legacy bare root-key file SecretRefs when the JSON payload has that exact root property, while preserving JSON-pointer schema/parity checks

## Root Cause
Telegram used one per-chat/per-thread sequential lane for all inbound updates, so follow-up text sent during an active run waited for the current handler to finish before it could affect the run. The first patch fixed the direct grammY sequentializer, but isolated polling uses its own durable spool drain and was still deriving lanes with the old serialized key. Separately, older managed secrets configs can contain file SecretRef ids that name root JSON keys directly; newer runtime resolution required JSON pointers and could fail gateway startup before users had a chance to migrate.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts src/secrets/resolve.test.ts src/secrets/exec-secret-ref-id-parity.test.ts src/config/config.secrets-schema.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/active-run-ingress.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-core.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-reply-fence.ts src/secrets/resolve.ts src/secrets/resolve.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `npm pack --pack-destination /tmp/openclaw-local-pack.z29LPG --json --loglevel=error`
- `openclaw update --tag /tmp/openclaw-local-pack.z29LPG/openclaw-2026.5.30.tgz --json --yes --timeout 1800`
- `openclaw health --json`

## Notes
- Latest upstream already treats ChatGPT/Codex auth as the `openai` provider with `CODEX_API_KEY`; I did not carry the older local `openai-codex` env-var hunk into this PR.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw gateway startup recovered from the stale file-backed SecretRef compatibility failure, and Telegram/Slack channel health remained live after the compatibility repair. The Telegram active-run ingress behavior is covered by focused tests for both the direct Telegram middleware path and the isolated-spool polling path; commands/media remain on the normal serialized lane.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw setup on Hanson's Mini, OpenClaw runtime `2026.5.30` from this branch (`b3fc0c2`), gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`, Telegram polling accounts `clawd2` through `clawd6` plus `default`, Slack `compbioatberkeley`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Packed this branch locally, installed it with `openclaw update --tag /tmp/openclaw-local-pack.z29LPG/openclaw-2026.5.30.tgz --json --yes --timeout 1800`, reinstalled the node LaunchAgent after launchd returned a transient bootstrap error, then ran `openclaw gateway status --json`, `openclaw status --json`, `openclaw gateway call channels.status --json --timeout 30000 --params '{}'`, and `openclaw health --json`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output, summarized and redacted to omit secret values:
```json
{
  "version": "OpenClaw 2026.5.30 (b3fc0c2)",
  "gateway": {
    "rpc": { "ok": true, "version": "2026.5.30" },
    "runtime": { "status": "running", "state": "active" }
  },
  "node": {
    "runtime": { "status": "running", "state": "active" },
    "serviceVersion": "2026.5.30"
  },
  "status": {
    "taskAudit": { "total": 0, "warnings": 0, "errors": 0 },
    "activeTasks": 0,
    "secretDiagnostics": []
  },
  "telegram": [
    { "accountId": "clawd2", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "clawd3", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "clawd4", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "clawd5", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "clawd6", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" },
    { "accountId": "default", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "mode": "polling" }
  ],
  "telegramConfig": {
    "streaming": "partial",
    "queue": { "mode": "steer", "byChannel": { "telegram": "steer" } }
  }
}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Gateway RPC returned `ok: true`, both gateway and node services were running on the updated package, `openclaw health --json` returned `ok: true`, all six Telegram accounts were connected with no last error, task audit had `0` warnings/errors, no pending Telegram ingress-spool `.json` or `.processing` files were present, and the installed dist contains the shared `active-run-ingress` lane code.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live long-running Telegram task and inject a mid-task follow-up from Telegram; that behavior is covered by focused automated tests in this PR.
target.author: hilo-hilo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
